global.title: Iran wants Strait of Hormuz reopening tied to US ending the war, officials say. Follow live updates. - The Boston Globe
global.summary: Iran is linking any reopening of the Strait of Hormuz to an end to the war, while international pressure grows over the waterway and regional casualties continue to rise.
global.whyItMatters: This remains a major flashpoint shaping prayer, preaching, and pastoral care around fear, peace, and global instability.

global.title: South Sudan says 14 killed in plane crash near Juba - Reuters
global.summary: South Sudan says 14 people were killed in a plane crash near Juba, adding to the burdens facing a fragile nation already marked by instability and humanitarian need.
global.whyItMatters: It is a reminder to pray for grieving families and for vulnerable nations where tragedy compounds existing hardship.

global.title: Australia to levy Big Tech unless local news deals struck - Reuters
global.summary: Australia is moving to impose a levy on major tech firms unless they reach agreements supporting local news, signaling a fresh push to protect public-interest journalism.
global.whyItMatters: Pastors depend on trustworthy information ecosystems; this story touches truth-telling, media stewardship, and civic trust.

canadian.title: Champagne spring - Politico
canadian.summary: Ottawa committees are tackling defense, immigration, fisheries, and public accounts, while Dominic LeBlanc says 275 projects have already been submitted to the Major Projects Office.
canadian.whyItMatters: Federal priorities around infrastructure, immigration, and defense shape the social and economic pressures churches will feel locally.

canadian.title: A new study looked into the effectiveness of the COVID-19 drug Paxlovid. Here’s what the results showed. - CTV News
canadian.summary: A new study reported by CTV says Paxlovid may not reduce hospitalization or death but may help some people recover more quickly, as public health concerns continue alongside measles reports.
canadian.whyItMatters: Church leaders still help people navigate health anxiety, trust, and care for vulnerable members with wisdom and calm.

canadian.title: VIDEO: Confederation College and Alstom partnership aims to strengthen workforce - TBNewsWatch.com
canadian.summary: Confederation College and Alstom are partnering to strengthen workforce development, highlighting a practical investment in skills and employment pathways.
canadian.whyItMatters: Stories of training and dignified work matter pastorally for families, young adults, and communities seeking stability.

persecutedChurch.title: Israel appoints special envoy to Christian world after scandals strain ties - The Times of Israel
persecutedChurch.summary: Israel has appointed its first special envoy to the Christian world after recent incidents strained relations and drew criticism from Christians globally.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a meaningful development for prayer and awareness around Christian vulnerability, witness, and diplomacy in the Holy Land.

persecutedChurch.title: David Rosen: Israel doesn’t adequately understand Christian relations - The Times of Israel
persecutedChurch.summary: Rabbi David Rosen argues Israel lacks a serious long-term strategy for relations with churches, after outrage over an image of a soldier smashing a statue of Jesus in Lebanon.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It offers important context for understanding how Christian communities can be marginalized even amid broader geopolitical conflict.

leadership.title: How leaders can avoid falling short of staff expectations during transformations - Consultancy.uk
leadership.summary: A new leadership piece argues that change efforts fail when leaders hedge their commitment, ignore data, or underestimate staff expectations and change fatigue.
leadership.whyItMatters: Helpful for church leadership: people follow courageous, transparent leaders who align vision, communication, and real care for those affected.

goodNews.title: ‘Coming home’: kiwi enter parliament for first time as New Zealand marks conservation win - The Guardian
goodNews.summary: Kiwi birds entered New Zealand’s parliament for the first time as conservationists celebrated a successful reintroduction effort with remarkable chick survival rates.
goodNews.whyItMatters: A hopeful picture of restoration, patient stewardship, and what can happen when communities work together for renewal.
